title: Kill Chain Mapping Exercise
when_to_use: When analysing any incident or attack scenario

steps.title: Select a Breach Report
steps.instruction: Read a public breach report (e.g., from Krebs on Security or Mandiant)
steps.tip: Start with well-documented incidents — they provide the most detail for mapping

steps.title: List Attacker Actions
steps.instruction: List every attacker action mentioned in the report
steps.tip: Be exhaustive — even minor actions (port scanning, credential testing) count

steps.title: Create Kill Chain Table
steps.instruction: Create a 7-column table using Kill Chain phases: Recon, Weaponise, Deliver, Exploit, Install, C2, Actions
steps.tip: Some actions may span multiple phases — place them in the primary phase

steps.title: Map Actions to Phases
steps.instruction: Map each action to the correct phase
steps.tip: Common confusion: Delivery vs Exploitation. Delivery is how the payload reaches the target; Exploitation is when it executes

steps.title: Identify Detection Points
steps.instruction: Identify which phase was first detected and which went undetected
steps.tip: Most organisations detect late (C2 or Actions phase). Earlier detection = better security posture

steps.title: Suggest Improvements
steps.instruction: Suggest one detection improvement per undetected phase
steps.tip: Be specific: 'implement email attachment sandboxing' is better than 'improve email security'
